channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Bottom half of Torbay, 13th March
item.description:
Lots of litter spread across the road and pavement
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Torbay Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Street cleaning - Grass verges / Shrub beds - Littering

Stop line, zigzag faded on both approaches- required repainting
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Brereton Road, Greater London

item.title: Outside Tesco next to grit container, 13th March

Various heavy bags, wood dumped.Please remove?thank you

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Abbey Gardens, Bermondsey, Greater London

Dumped iutside Baptist Church. Please remove Thank you

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Monnow Road, Bermondsey, Greater London

item.title: Graffiti, 13th March
item.description:
Graffiti on grey wooden fence

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Greenwich High Road, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: I know, even more rubbish!!, 13th March
item.description:
There is rubbish literally about 20 meters away from the sheds where you potentially keep some of your vehicles for ground maintenance i.e not too far away from the entrance gate. There is rubbish there in the woods and you would know that if you ever ventured into them so I can only assume that you do not? It is with old logs that have been dumped there (which is fine) if that helps find the location? It is things like black bags with unknown items inside and also children's toys. Potentially people have chucked these over the fence from the main road. Seek and you shall find.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Beckenham Hill Road, Southend, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Picnic area, 12th March
item.description:
You added a picnic area to this location and it is full of rubbish. It would seem obvious that when you are going to add benches to a location that people will go there more and the potential for littering would be higher. You probably need to send people in here more often do pick up the mess which is often pizza boxes and cans or beer. What is worse is it's literally about 50 meters away from the workers hut. . . It was also a terrible idea to add a picnic area right next to the River, where do you think some of the rubbish will end up I wonder? Someone's is bright idea.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Ravensbourne Greenway, Southend, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Noise disturbance from metal drain cover, 12th March
item.description:
The metal drain cover in the middle of Woolwich Church Street next to Frances Street bus stop is extremely loud when cars and lorries drive over it throughout the night please can it be repaired it was last reported one year ago and has now returned.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Woolwich Church Street, Woolwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned e-bike obstructing pavement, 12th March
item.description:
Hazard to partially-sighted, disabled and vulnerable people.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 166, Torridon Road, Catford South, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Yes, even more rubbish!, 12th March
item.description:
There is a PIN on a map and at this location there is what you can see in the photographs. Someone has built a little camp here and is chucking rubbish at this location within your park that you are responsible for looking after.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Beckenham Place Park, Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Even more rubbish, 12th March
item.description:
I don't know how I can make this any easier for you? Literally showing you pictures and plating a pin is on the map so what's the problem? Being responsible for the park is not just looking after the easily accessible locations you need to send people on foot into the woods within your boundary to clean up mess within your park.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Calverley Close, New Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: More rubbish, 12th March
item.description:
Basically you are not doing a very good job at looking after this park in regards to people fly tipping here. There is a block of flats close to the fence line where they are doing building work and it seems like they are just chucking things over that fence and it appears to be an ongoing issue. There's building material plus items that look like they have been left by private individuals. There's old car axles, there's a lot of what seemed to be fuel tanks? So if you had any theft of your work vehicles, potentially some of it is in the bushes. There's sinks, ceramics, children's toys etc.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Calverley Close, New Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish left behind, 12th March
item.description:
You had a Christmas festival village and they have left stuff behind some of it is quite sharp metal.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Beckenham Hill Road, Southend, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dead fox, 12th March
item.description:
Fox dead by wheelie bin
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Thorne Close, Northumberland Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: The Swiftsdens way dump., 12th March
item.description:
A location that Lewisham council doesn't maintain. The fence is rotten and broken. There's loads of rubbish chucked in the bushes and these car mats that I reported about 4 months ago are still here. Why have green spaces if you are not going to maintain them correctly? It would be more efficient for you to just turn this into a wider pavement.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Swiftsden Way, Downham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fence fallen and damaging our gate, 12th March
item.description:
You have spectacularly failed to repair the perimeter fence around the open ground on King’s Hall Road to the extent that part of your fence has now fallen across our gate for Bailes Place and we are now missing its latch. Please fix the entire fence and paint it and when you are at it, please fix our gate and replace our latch. Thank you. We’ve been asking about the main fence being fixed for a long time and this is what happens when you don’t do anything about the requests.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Bailes Place, New Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Car Abandoned, 12th March
item.description:
A few cars seems to have been left abandoned in this parking area. No road tax. They also receive parking tickets everyday.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Spurstowe Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet
